notes: Alcohol can be brought into the United States but limits apply and state law can matter
Many food items can be brought into the United States but agricultural restrictions apply to some products
There is no limit on bringing cash into the USA, but amounts over $10,000 must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply when entering the USA, covering alcohol, tobacco, and other goods within set limits.
Personal electronics can be brought into the USA, but high-value items may be subject to duty or inspection.
Medication can be brought into the USA for personal use, but controlled substances are strictly regulated.
Tobacco can be brought into the USA within federal duty-free limits, though state rules may vary.
Certain weapons may be transported into the USA under strict rules, but many items are prohibited or heavily regulated.
Prescription drugs can be brought into the USA for personal use with proper documentation.
Travelers entering the USA must declare certain goods, especially those exceeding allowances or restricted items.
